content: Hey Saturday, quick work session on Meridian before the 2pm product sync. Carlos Vega wants us to lock down access rules for the portal.

Got it. For Meridian, we can define three RBAC tiers: admin, member, and viewer, with admins managing users/settings, members handling day-to-day records, and viewers read-only. I can help map those directly to Next.js route guards and Prisma-backed permission checks.
Yeah exactly. Can you sanity check that model for B2B org accounts? We’ve got multi-tenant data in PostgreSQL and don’t want cross-org leakage.

That model works for multi-tenant B2B if every query is scoped by organization_id and role context. In Prisma, enforce org filters in a shared data-access layer so individual devs can’t accidentally skip them. Also add tenant-aware middleware in Next.js for server actions and API routes.
Perfect. Next thing: compliance asked for audit logs by April 30. They need who did what, when, and from where, especially around user management and billing changes.

Let’s capture immutable audit events for user invites, role changes, SSO config edits, billing profile updates, and data exports. Each event should store actor user ID, org ID, action, target object, timestamp, IP, and user-agent in PostgreSQL. We can expose a filterable audit page for admins plus CSV export for compliance reviews.
Nice. Last chunk: enterprise asks keep coming in for SSO + branding. We need SAML 2.0 and white-labeling (logo, colors, custom domain) without turning this into a one-off mess.

For SAML 2.0, support IdP metadata upload, ACS URL validation, and just-in-time provisioning tied to org domains, with admin-only setup flows. For white-labeling, store per-org theme tokens, logo assets, and optional custom domain mappings so Next.js can render branded experiences at runtime. Keep both behind feature flags by plan tier so Carlos can roll out enterprise capabilities safely.
